events: FOMC minutes (June 16-17 meeting) release TODAY at 2:00 PM ET — hawkish dot plot with 9/18 members projecting a rate hike
Q2 earnings season begins this week: PepsiCo Jul 9, Delta Jul 10, major banks (JPM, BAC, C, WFC) Jul 14
CPI June 2026 release Tuesday Jul 14 at 8:30 AM ET
rates: on hold 3.50-3.75%; hawkish tilt — 9 of 18 FOMC members project at least one hike by year-end 2026
note: S&P 500 futures down ~1% this morning after Trump declared the US-Iran ceasefire over, triggering a 5%+ surge in oil prices. This adds an inflation risk just as the Fed is already hawkish. FOMC minutes from the June meeting drop today at 2 PM ET — the primary clue to the rate path since new Fed Chair Warsh gave no forward guidance at the meeting. Dot plot shows 9/18 officials project at least one hike; median PCE forecast raised to 3.6%. Market CAPE ratio sits at 39.9 (125% above its historic average). None of our thesis candidates carry a positive margin of safety at current prices. Geopolitical uncertainty, hot inflation, possible Fed hike, and stretched valuations all argue for holding cash and demanding a larger discount.
